cases.description: Bill 5056: a mid-sentence list where the real continuation ('if available...') follows immediately with no comma — STOP-4's bare-word check must catch it, not swallow it into the list.
cases.sentence: The applicant shall submit a description of the firearm including the make, model, caliber and manufacturer's number if available at the time of applying for the purchase of the firearm.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including the make, model, caliber and manufacturer's number

cases.description: Bill 5176: 'payment including retainage is not made' — the bare verb 'is' (no comma before it) ends the list right after the single named item.
cases.sentence: If payment including retainage is not made within thirty days, the contractor may suspend work under the contract.

cases.description: Bill 1388: 'other states including Pennsylvania and Virginia also allow...' — the bare 'also allow' (no comma before it) ends the list before the real continuation.
cases.sentence: The department shall study whether other states including Pennsylvania and Virginia also allow similar licensing reciprocity agreements.

cases.description: Bill 1171: 'employees, including student employees, of academic...' — STOP-3's 'of' connector ends the list at the second comma.
cases.sentence: The university shall provide training to employees, including student employees, of academic departments within ninety days.

cases.description: Bill 1894: '...tribes that are based in Washington but that now reside in Oregon, to incorporate...' — the 'but that' exception absorbs the description instead of stopping, and the real stop is STOP-3's 'to' connector at the next comma.
cases.sentence: The commission shall consult with Indian tribes, including tribes that are based in Washington but that now reside in Oregon, to incorporate their input into the plan.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including tribes that are based in Washington but that now reside in Oregon

cases.description: Bill 2020: 'including any hospital that comes within... if the hospital is also licensed...' — the relative-clause shield ('that') blocks every bare-verb stop in the clause (is/also licensed/if), so the list runs to the sentence's own period.
cases.sentence: The department shall regulate hospitals, including any hospital that comes within the definition of a critical access hospital if the hospital is also licensed as a rural health clinic.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including any hospital that comes within the definition of a critical access hospital if the hospital is also licensed as a rural health clinic

cases.description: Bill 1921: 'including its make, model, vehicle identification number, type of body, and power to be used' — the coordinated to-be guard (', and power to be used') prevents an early stop at 'be', so the full list survives to the period.
cases.sentence: The registrant shall report the vehicle's specifications, including its make, model, vehicle identification number, type of body, and power to be used for commercial purposes.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including its make, model, vehicle identification number, type of body, and power to be used for commercial purposes

cases.description: Bill 1267: 'excluding students... enrolled in kindergarten, multiplied by...' — STOP-2's 'multiplied' resumption word ends the list at the comma.
cases.sentence: The office shall calculate enrollment excluding students who are enrolled in kindergarten, multiplied by the funding rate per student.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: excluding students who are enrolled in kindergarten

cases.description: Bill 5601: 'including whether biomethane availability is sufficient...' — the 'whether' shield blocks the bare-verb stop at 'is', so the real stop is the semicolon (STOP-1), not the relative clause's own verb.
cases.sentence: The commission shall evaluate factors including whether biomethane availability is sufficient to meet demand; the commission shall also consider cost impacts on ratepayers.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including whether biomethane availability is sufficient to meet demand

cases.description: Bill 1000 utility-employee sentence: 'including: (i) ... ; or (ii) ...' — the colon look-ahead detects the sublist even though other clause text (who/is) sits between the marker and the colon-triggering point isn't relevant here; the lettered items run to the sentence's own end.
cases.sentence: The court shall consider whether the current offense involved the assault of a utility employee of any publicly or privately owned utility company or agency, who is at the time of the act engaged in official duties, including: (i) The maintenance or repair of utility poles, lines, conduits, pipes, or other infrastructure; or (ii) connecting, disconnecting, or recording utility meters.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including: (i) The maintenance or repair of utility poles, lines, conduits, pipes, or other infrastructure; or (ii) connecting, disconnecting, or recording utility meters.

cases.description: A parenthetical case — the list is exactly the text inside the parentheses, not including the parens themselves.
cases.sentence: The facility must provide treatment services (including full-time residential treatment) to eligible patients.

cases.description: A 'not including' case — Gate 1 classifies it REVERSED and no boundary is computed; the instruction is unaffected.
cases.sentence: The tenant may use the storage area, not including the rental of cold storage lockers, unless otherwise agreed in writing.

cases.description: A verb-use case — 'cost of including' is not a list at all (Gate 2), since the marker isn't followed by a filler phrase.
cases.sentence: The agency may recover the additional cost of including such service in the annual budget.

cases.description: Bill 1121: 'including but not limited to core plus programs' — the filler phrase is absorbed as part of the list start, and the list runs to the sentence's own end.
cases.sentence: The board shall fund innovative programs, including but not limited to core plus programs.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including but not limited to core plus programs

cases.description: A sentence with two genuinely separate markers — inclusionLists must hold both entries, in order, each independently bounded.
cases.sentence: The district shall serve all residents, including part-time staff, if funding is available, excluding volunteers who serve fewer than ten hours per month.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: excluding volunteers who serve fewer than ten hours per month

cases.description: A plain sentence with no 'including'/'excluding' marker at all, guarding against a future change producing spurious detections.
cases.sentence: The department shall submit an annual report to the legislature by June 30th of each year.

cases.description: Bill 1433: 'shall include, but not be limited to: (a) Establishing requirements...' — bare 'include' is never a marker (only the words 'including'/'excluding' are), and Gate 0 additionally confirms this occurrence is the sentence's own verb (immediately preceded by the modal 'shall') rather than a marker candidate at all. No list is detected; the unit's action retains the full 'include, but not be limited to: (a) ...' content untouched, since nothing was excised.
cases.sentence: The rules adopted by the department shall include, but not be limited to: (a) Establishing requirements for licensure.

cases.description: Bill 1166: 'may accept ... including but not limited to nonprofit entities ..., and may receive, utilize, and dispose of same ...' — a same-actor compound sentence that renders as two clauses (renderer.js's splitCompoundAction), with the detected list belonging to the first ('may accept') clause. At the pipeline level (what this suite checks) there is exactly one detection, correctly bounded past the coordinate split point rather than truncated by it. Renderer-level verification (the rendered note attaching to the first clause's paragraph, not the second, and the filler 'but not limited to' being stripped from that note) was confirmed separately by direct execution against renderISC/renderUnit, since that is outside what runPipeline's tetherAnchor exposes.
cases.sentence: The department may accept any and all donations and grants of money, equipment, supplies, materials, and services from any third party, including but not limited to nonprofit entities and community-based postsecondary education programs, and may receive, utilize, and dispose of same in accordance with the terms of the donation or grant.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including but not limited to nonprofit entities and community-based postsecondary education programs

cases.description: Same bill 1166 sentence as bill-1166-coordinate-split-list-detection, anchoring the filler-stripping fix specifically: the pipeline's recorded listText legitimately retains the filler phrase 'but not limited to' as part of the detected span (that's the validated rule's boundary, unchanged here) — but the renderer's displayed note strips it, reading 'Named examples: nonprofit entities and community-based postsecondary education programs' with no filler words. Confirmed by direct execution against renderISC/renderUnit; not checkable via tetherAnchor alone, which is why this entry's own expectedInclusionLists matches the pipeline-level (filler-retained) span exactly like the sibling case above.
cases.sentence: The department may accept any and all donations and grants of money, equipment, supplies, materials, and services from any third party, including but not limited to nonprofit entities and community-based postsecondary education programs, and may receive, utilize, and dispose of same in accordance with the terms of the donation or grant.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including but not limited to nonprofit entities and community-based postsecondary education programs

cases.description: Real bill 1433 text, verbatim, with the nested 'including:' inside lettered item (a) that a previous version's colon-sublist extraction over-consumed: 'shall include, but not be limited to:' at the top level is correctly not a marker (Gate 0), but the nested 'including:' after '(a) Establishing requirements for an administration session' is a real marker producing a COLON_SUBLIST detection. This specific excerpt's extraction happens to leave a non-empty action and does not itself reduce the unit count, so it is a straightforward regression anchor confirming the real text still parses this way. The fail-open invariant this case is named for (extraction is skipped, with the list still recorded as extracted: false, whenever removing a list's span would reduce the sentence's unit count or leave a resulting unit's action empty/effectively-empty) was exercised and confirmed separately by direct execution against a fuller reconstruction of this same structure with an embedded second obligation inside the lettered item, since that specific failure mode needs more surrounding text than this excerpt alone provides, and unit counts/action text are not fields this suite's script checks.
cases.sentence:
The rules adopted by the department shall include, but not be limited to:
(a) Establishing requirements for an administration session, including:
(i) The form and content of client information to be collected by the facilitator;
(ii) Procedures for reporting information to the department.

expectedInclusionLists.listText: including: (i) The form and content of client information to be collected by the facilitator; (ii) Procedures for reporting information to the department.
